canonical_definition: An extension of the GLUE benchmark specifically designed to evaluate the out-of-domain robustness and generalization of language models.

relationships.paper: Supervised Knowledge Makes Large Language Models Better In-context Learners
relationships.evidence: We conduct experiments on both zero-shot and few-shot settings of natural language understanding and question answering (QA). SuperContext is validated on a comprehensive OOD benchmarks GLUE-X (Yang et al., 2022), and a QA dataset, SQuAD 2.0 (Rajpurkar et al., 2018). (Section 2)

iclr24: Supervised Knowledge Makes Large Language Models Better In-context Learners

relationships.paper: Supervised Knowledge Makes Large Language Models Better In-context Learners
relationships.evidence: In particular, we consider 7 classical NLU tasks: Sentiment Analysis (SA), Natural Language Inference (NLI), Paraphrasing, Question-Answering NLI (QNLI), Textual Entailment, Textual Similarity, and Linguistic Acceptability (Grammar). (Section 3.1)
